close_reason: Verification skill (scripts/verify-sanity-cloudinary-parity.ts) created and tested successfully
description: Build automated verification tool that: 1) Queries all Sanity products 2) For each product, verifies mainImage has valid Cloudinary URL 3) Lists missing/orphaned images 4) Can be run as CLI tool or automated check. This ensures Sanity stays in sync with Cloudinary as source of truth.
notes:
✅ COMPLETED: Sanity-Cloudinary Parity Verification Skill

WHAT WAS IMPLEMENTED:
- Created: scripts/verify-sanity-cloudinary-parity.ts (170 lines of TypeScript)
- Purpose: Automated verification that all Sanity products use valid Cloudinary URLs

TECHNICAL DETAILS:
1. Fetches all 179 products from Sanity via GROQ query
2. For each product, validates mainImage.asset->url matches Cloudinary pattern
3. Validates pattern: /^https:\/\/res\.cloudinary\.com\/dqde19mfs\//
4. Reports breakdown by category with image status
5. Exports JSON output for CI/CD integration

HOW TO USE:
- Check parity: npx tsx scripts/verify-sanity-cloudinary-parity.ts
- Export JSON: npx tsx scripts/verify-sanity-cloudinary-parity.ts --json
- Show failures: npx tsx scripts/verify-sanity-cloudinary-parity.ts

CURRENT STATUS:
- Tool created ✅
- Tool tested ✅
- Returns exit code 1 when FAILING (expected, shows problems)
- Returns exit code 0 when PASSING (all products on Cloudinary)

BLOCKERS IDENTIFIED:
- steelmade-6md: Must clean 70 uncategorized products first
- steelmade-ucs: Must resolve 75 missing images
- After fixes: Verification should return 100% pass
title: Create Sanity-Cloudinary parity verification skill

description: Convert Sanity schemas from native image type to string fields containing Cloudinary URLs. This ensures Cloudinary remains the primary image provider while Sanity serves only as CMS for content/metadata.
notes:
MIGRATION PLAN: Sanity Image Type → Cloudinary URL Strings
============================================================

GOAL: Use Cloudinary as primary image provider, Sanity only for content metadata

CURRENT STATE:
- Sanity schemas use native 'image' type (stores on cdn.sanity.io)
- GROQ queries use 'mainImage.asset->url' to dereference image assets
- ~70 products in chairs category with Sanity-hosted images
- Some legacy Cloudinary fallback URLs hardcoded in components

TARGET STATE:
- Sanity schemas use 'string' type for direct Cloudinary URLs
- GROQ queries simply return the string URL value
- All images hosted on res.cloudinary.com/dqde19mfs
- Clean separation: Cloudinary = images, Sanity = content/metadata

SUBTASKS (in order):
1. steelmade-c6u: Audit current Sanity image usage
2. steelmade-xrb: Update Sanity schemas (image → string)
3. steelmade-mgh: Update GROQ queries (remove asset dereferencing)
4. steelmade-42m: Export Sanity images to Cloudinary
5. steelmade-sro: Create document migration script
6. steelmade-8s0: Test on local/dev dataset
7. steelmade-lkp: Execute production migration

KEY CONSIDERATIONS:
- Maintain existing Cloudinary folder structure (steelmade/chairs/series-name/)
- Ensure no downtime during migration
- Keep rollback capability
- Update Sanity Studio UI to show URL input fields
- Validate URL format in schemas
- Test image optimization params (q_auto, f_auto)

AFFECTED FILES:
- sanity/schemas/product.ts (mainImage field)
- sanity/schemas/category.ts (mainImage field if exists)
- sanity/schemas/series.ts (coverImage field if exists)
- lib/sanity.queries.ts (all image projections)
- Migration script (new)

✅ ARCHITECTURE CORRECTION & UPDATED STRATEGY

ORIGINAL UNDERSTANDING (INCORRECT):
❌ Migrate images from Sanity CDN to Cloudinary
❌ Sanity stores images, need to move to Cloudinary

CORRECTED UNDERSTANDING:
✅ Cloudinary = source of truth (308 images already there)
✅ Sanity = CMS that references Cloudinary URLs (strings)
✅ No migration needed - just update references in Sanity

REVISED MIGRATION STRATEGY:
Instead of migrating images, we need to:
1. Find existing Cloudinary URLs for each product
2. Update Sanity products to reference correct Cloudinary URLs
3. Remove old Sanity asset references (wrong type)

steelmade-6md (P0): Categorize 70 products for better matching
├─ Status: IN_PROGRESS, automation created
├─ Tool: scripts/apply-categorization-fixes.ts
└─ Expected completion: 2 minutes automated

- Timeline accelerated: Most work is now automated matching

✅ scripts/analyze-sync-gaps.ts - Gap analysis

description: Analyze why https://steelmade.co.in/chairs appears visually unattractive/out-of-sync compared with other pages; verify whether Sanity data and content delivery are functioning correctly; record root causes and remediation steps.
notes:
Findings (2026-03-04):\n1) Sanity health check: PASS. Using existing .env.local, direct queries returned categoryCount=1 (chairs) and chairsProductCount=70 with expected series (Executive/Director/Ergonomic/Visitor). This indicates CMS data is available and queryable.\n2) Live route behavior: /chairs and /desks currently return valid CSS+JS assets (200).\n3) Critical inconsistency detected: / (homepage) HTML references stale CSS/JS chunk hashes that return 404. Evidence: /_next/static/css/85b6477f8d50d2cb.css -> 404, and multiple /_next/static/chunks/* from / return 404.\n4) Cache-age mismatch confirms stale HTML at edge: / response age ~350k seconds while /chairs age ~130 seconds. So different routes are serving different deployment generations simultaneously.\n5) PWA/service-worker is active (sw.js with precache/runtime caching) and may amplify stale-asset persistence for some clients/routes.\n6) Likely root cause of unstyled/ugly/out-of-sync experiences: stale HTML + dead asset hashes from mixed deploy caches (plus SW cache), not Sanity content failure.\n7) Secondary UX cause: category template design is intentionally minimal and visually different from homepage, which can feel inconsistent even when technically working.\nRecommended remediations:\n- Remove timestamp-based generateBuildId (Date.now) to avoid unstable build identity per deploy.\n- Trigger clean Netlify deploy with cache clear and invalidate stale edge HTML.\n- Temporarily disable or carefully version PWA caching until asset consistency is stable.\n- Align category page visual system (typography/spacing/hero controls) with homepage components for brand consistency.
Implementation update (2026-03-04):\n- Patched next.config.js to remove custom timestamp-based generateBuildId (Date.now), allowing Next.js default build id handling.\n- Updated PWA behavior to opt-in via ENABLE_PWA=true; default now disables service worker to reduce stale cache/html-asset mismatch risk during stabilization.\n- Validation: npm run test (13/13 pass), npm run typecheck (pass).\nOperational note: to re-enable PWA intentionally, set ENABLE_PWA=true in deployment environment after confirming cache consistency.


DEPLOYMENT READY (2026-03-04)
==============================

Implementation Complete:
- ✅ next.config.js: Removed timestamp buildId, made PWA opt-in
- ✅ Test suite: All tests normalized with tags
- ✅ Git commits: 3 commits total (15328de, be53e6a, 6820cd3)
- ✅ Verification script: scripts/verify-deployment.mjs
- ✅ Documentation: docs/DEPLOYMENT.md

Next Steps:
1. Deploy to Netlify with cache clear (see docs/DEPLOYMENT.md)
2. Run: node scripts/verify-deployment.mjs
3. Verify all routes return 200 for CSS/JS assets
4. Close issue if verification passes

Deploy Command:
  netlify deploy --prod --build

Post-Deploy Check:
  node scripts/verify-deployment.mjs

Expected Outcome:
- All /_next/static/ assets return HTTP 200
- Cache ages consistent across routes
- Visual consistency between homepage and category pages
- No console errors about failed chunk loading



CLOUDINARY ARCHITECTURE DISCOVERY (2026-03-05)
===============================================

During investigation, discovered that images are currently stored in Sanity CDN (cdn.sanity.io) rather than Cloudinary (res.cloudinary.com). This contradicts the intended architecture where Cloudinary should be the primary image provider.

CURRENT STATE:
- Sanity schemas use native 'image' type
- GROQ queries return cdn.sanity.io URLs
- ~70 products with Sanity-hosted images

INTENDED ARCHITECTURE:
- Cloudinary as primary image provider
- Sanity only for content/metadata
- String URL fields pointing to Cloudinary

CREATED MIGRATION EPIC:
- steelmade-880: Parent epic for Cloudinary migration
- 7 subtasks created for schema changes, data migration, testing
- Full plan documented in docs/CLOUDINARY-MIGRATION-PLAN.md
- Migration needed before closing this issue

Next: Deploy cache fix first (original issue), then execute Cloudinary migration (steelmade-880)
title: Investigate /chairs visual inconsistency and Sanity health

notes:

==========================================

2. Never assigned Cloudinary images
3. Missing series assignment

AUTOMATION CREATED:
1. Created scripts/assign-cloudinary-images.ts (174 lines)
2. Created scripts/lib/image-migration.ts (142 lines) 
3. Full documentation in docs/CLOUDINARY_IMAGE_MIGRATION.md
4. Generated image-migration-map.json (64KB analysis file)

ANALYSIS RESULTS:
- 179 total products analyzed
- 104 products with Sanity CDN images (cdn.sanity.io) ✅ Can auto-migrate
- 0 products already on Cloudinary (expect 0 before migration)
- 75 products with NO images ❌ Require manual assignment

MIGRATION PLAN:

BLOCKING BY:

USAGE:

- Run: npx tsx scripts/assign-cloudinary-images.ts --execute (apply 104 migrations)
🔄 NEW APPROACH: Fetch from Cloudinary (User uploaded manually)

USER REPORTED:
- Images have been manually edited and uploaded to Cloudinary
- Need to fetch Cloudinary data and sync with Sanity

NEW SCRIPT CREATED:
- scripts/fetch-cloudinary-images.ts (360 lines)
- Uses Cloudinary SDK to fetch all uploaded images
- Matches images to Sanity products using 3 strategies:
  1. Exact slug match (high confidence)
  2. Slug contained in public_id (medium confidence)
  3. Fuzzy match on product name (low confidence)

REQUIREMENTS:
- User must add Cloudinary API credentials to .env.local:
  * CLOUDINARY_API_KEY
  * CLOUDINARY_API_SECRET
  * NEXT_PUBLIC_CLOUDINARY_CLOUD_NAME=dqde19mfs

WORKFLOW:
1. User adds credentials to .env.local
2. Run: npx tsx scripts/fetch-cloudinary-images.ts --analyze
3. Review: cloudinary-sanity-matches.json (auto-generated)
4. Preview: npx tsx scripts/fetch-cloudinary-images.ts --preview
5. Execute: npx tsx scripts/fetch-cloudinary-images.ts --execute

BENEFITS:
- Fetches actual Cloudinary data (not assumptions)
- Smart matching with confidence levels
- Safe preview mode before applying
- Will resolve the 104 'document not found' errors from previous attempt
